description.en: A spongy, sour teff flatbread fermented for days until wild yeasts riddle the batter with bubbles, then steamed on a wide griddle into a tangy, pliable round. It doubles as plate, cutlery and tablecloth under Ethiopian and Eritrean stews.
description.de: Ein schwammiges, säuerliches Teff-Fladenbrot, tagelang fermentiert, bis wilde Hefen den Teig durchsetzen, dann dampfgegart zu einer würzigen, biegsamen Scheibe. Dient äthiopischen und eritreischen Eintöpfen als Teller, Besteck und Tischdecke.
description.es: Un pan plano esponjoso y ácido de teff, fermentado durante días hasta que las levaduras silvestres llenan la masa de burbujas, luego cocido al vapor en una plancha ancha hasta formar un disco flexible y agrio. Sirve de plato, cubiertos y mantel bajo los guisos etíopes y eritreos.
description.fr: Un pain plat spongieux et acidulé au teff, fermenté des jours durant jusqu'à cribler la pâte de bulles, puis cuit à la vapeur en un disque souple. Il sert d'assiette, de couverts et de nappe sous les ragoûts éthiopiens et érythréens.

origin.note: The shared national staple of both Ethiopia and Eritrea; teff blends and fermentation length vary by region and household.

ingredients.note: whole grain teff, brown or ivory
stirred in just before cooking, not during fermentation
optional ersho (starter from a previous batch) to kickstart fermentation

keeping.note: Stays pliable several days at room temperature wrapped in cloth; refrigerates over a week and freezes well, though it's tangiest and spongiest fresh.

method.title: Mix the batter
method.summary: Whisk teff flour with water (and a spoonful of ersho starter if you have one) into a smooth, thin batter, thinner than pancake batter.

method.title: Ferment
method.summary: Cover loosely and leave at warm room temperature for 2-5 days, until it smells pleasantly sour and is full of tiny bubbles.

method.title: Season
method.summary: Stir in the salt just before cooking; the fermentation itself is done without salt.

method.title: Pour & spread
method.summary: Pour a ladleful onto a hot, lightly greased wide griddle or non-stick pan, swirling quickly to spread it thin and even.

method.title: Steam-cook
method.summary: Cover with a lid and let it steam-cook on one side only until the surface is covered in tiny holes ('eyes') and the edges lift cleanly.

method.title: Cool flat
method.summary: Slide onto a clean cloth or tray to cool uncovered so it stays pliable rather than turning gummy.

source.adaptation: Cup measurements converted to grams using a standard teff-flour weight (~188 g/cup); hydration and salt percentage computed from those converted weights per baker's-percentage convention.
